title: Wano Splits Between Udon and the Flower Capital
description: On pages 14–17, this part focuses on Unknown person #13, Toko, Unknown person #14, Komurasaki, Unknown person #15, Unknown person #16 as The Wano rebellion spreads between Udon prison, the Flower Capital, and the first shocks around Komurasaki and Orochi.
scene: The rebellion’s pieces scatter across Wano while Luffy struggles in Udon and Orochi’s court grows unstable in the Flower Capital.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So you're the old geezer... that has been stalking komurasaki...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hey, Komura- Saki! This is a joke, right!?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...I sacrificed everything for you!! I don't have a single thing left!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: For your sake... I lost it all!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Here we go again. How many times has this happened now?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you cry in front of the shop, gramps.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: If you don't have any business here...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Then get lost, you perverted geezer!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Nano Country's No. I Prostitute THE COURTESAN, KONURASAKI

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: My house... my family... I exchanged all I had for money!!

dialogue.speaker: Komurasaki
dialogue.text: Look over here, Miss Courtesan- Co.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Give me back my money!! You thieving woman!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Her beauty is blinding!! I can't see!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: to ask someone to return some- thing that you have given them...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: to me, men are nothing more than dogs that bring me money....!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: without money, they are worthless to me.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: We won't let you escape this time.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Those without wealth have no right to live in the capital!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Those who live outside of the capital cannot even be called Human!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Damn it all!! Go Burn in Hell, Komura- SAK!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: The dayu, Komura- Saki, has arrived!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Momo, did you say you had a sister?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: Do you know who I am!?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Yes, I do have a sister....!! If she is still alive, then she should be 26 now.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even though there are people who witnessed my mother's death...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I haven't heard a thing about Hiyori's death.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: That she is alive some- where!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I see. It would be nice if you could meet her.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If Orochi were to find out about Hiyori...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: Then he would definitely target her!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Our meeting can only happen once we win this war!!

summary: This section advances the Wano - Udon and Flower Capital storyline with 64 dialogue beats. Luffy’s prison struggle, Big Mom’s amnesia, and the Flower Capital drama show Wano’s rebellion becoming harder to control.
